data.title: Shouhui Group Announces Passing of Non-Executive Director, Says Operations Unaffected
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Shouhui Group Limited ( (HK:2621) ).

Shouhui Group Limited has announced the passing of non-executive director Sirui Li on June 25, 2026, noting that he had served on the board since January 2024 and was recognized for his significant contributions, leadership and guidance to the group. The board conveyed deep condolences to his family and expressed appreciation for his impact on the company, while emphasizing that his death is not expected to have a material effect on the group’s operations, leaving overall business continuity and governance stability intact.

The company also confirmed the current composition of its board, led by executive director, chairman and chief executive officer Yao Guang, alongside a mix of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. This structure aims to maintain oversight and strategic direction following the loss of Mr. Li, signalling to investors and other stakeholders that Shouhui Group’s management and control framework remains robust despite the change in board membership.

More about Shouhui Group Limited

Shouhui Group Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2621. The group is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a conventional Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

Average Trading Volume: 278,010

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$758.4M

data.title: COSCO SHIPPING Energy Announces Final Dividend for 2025 and Details Tax Treatment
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co ( (HK:1138) ) has shared an update.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co., Ltd. declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.38 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, approved by shareholders on 26 June 2026. For Hong Kong H-shareholders, the dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars at HKD 0.43707 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.15019, with an ex-dividend date of 20 July 2026 and payment on 25 August 2026.

The company detailed the timetable for entitlement, including book closure from 22 to 27 July 2026 and a record date of 27 July 2026, and outlined differentiated withholding tax treatments for various categories of investors. The announcement underscores the firm’s ongoing shareholder return policy and clarifies tax obligations for resident, non-resident, and Stock Connect investors, which is important for income planning and compliance among its diverse shareholder base.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1138) stock is a Buy
with a HK$26.00 price target.

see the HK:1138 Stock Forecast page.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co., Ltd. operates in the energy shipping industry, focusing on the transportation of oil and related energy commodities. The company lists H shares in Hong Kong and serves both mainland Chinese and international investors, positioning itself as a major participant in cross-border capital markets for energy logistics.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co., Ltd., a major Chinese energy shipping company listed in Hong Kong, operates a fleet that supports global energy logistics and maintains H shares to tap international investors. Its governance structure and use of Hong Kong registrar services underscore a focus on orderly capital-market operations and shareholder engagement.

The company announced changes to the book closure period for its H share register and an earlier payment date for its proposed final dividend, adjusting the eligibility window for shareholders. These revisions slightly shift administrative timelines but reaffirm the dividend distribution, clarifying when investors must be on the H share register to qualify and when they will receive payment.

By resetting the closure period from 21–24 July to 22–27 July and moving the dividend payment forward from 28 August to 25 August, COSCO SHIPPING Energy refines its timetable without altering the substance of the payout. China Sandi Holdings has brought its financial reporting up to date, releasing its 2024 annual results and report and the full set of 2025 interim and annual results, meaning all disclosures required under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules have now been published. The latest update is out from Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2442) ).

Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited has announced changes to its board of directors and committee composition, underscoring the importance of independent oversight in its corporate governance framework. The company continues to align its governance practices with Hong Kong listing rules by rotating independent directors through critical board committees.

Independent non-executive director Dr. Gong Zhaolong has resigned due to personal commitments, stepping down from the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees. He is replaced by semiconductor industry veteran Dr. Gong Bin, who joins the board and the same three committees, bringing nearly two decades of sector experience and multiple policy and academic roles that may strengthen the Group’s strategic and compliance capabilities.

Dr. Gong Bin’s appointment is for an initial two-year term, subject to annual general meeting re-election and subsequent rotation at least once every three years under the company’s articles. His role, remunerated with an annual director’s fee of HK$180,000, is structured to preserve independence and is expected to support the company’s governance, risk oversight and long-term strategic positioning for shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited

Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through a group structure referred to as the Group. The company is overseen by a Board that relies on independent non-executive directors to staff key governance committees, including the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees, in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

The Group emphasizes compliance with the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited, particularly regarding director independence and board rotation. Director fees are benchmarked to qualifications, experience and prevailing market conditions, and reviewed periodically by the Remuneration Committee to maintain market-aligned governance standards.

Guming Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1364) ) has issued an announcement.

Guming Holdings Ltd. has completed the issuance of HK$1.96 billion zero-coupon guaranteed convertible bonds, with the instruments to be listed in Vienna and the conversion shares approved for trading in Hong Kong, placed with independent investors. The company plans to use the proceeds mainly for raw material and equipment procurement, capital structure enhancement through refinancing and share repurchases, and targeted investments in digitalisation, AI-driven tools and overseas expansion, marking a significant step in strengthening its balance sheet and operational technology.

The net proceeds will be allocated with about 90% directed to procurement and capital structure measures, while the remainder will fund emerging AI tools to streamline franchise and store management and predictive analytics to optimise site selection and product development. This allocation strategy is distinct from the use of funds from its previous global offering, indicating a shift toward more intensive operational optimisation and technology-led growth that could improve efficiency, support expansion and potentially enhance returns for shareholders.

data.title: Pa Shun Faces Receivership Over Key Pharma Assets After Scheme Default
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Pa Shun International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0574) ).

Pa Shun International Holdings has defaulted on a HK$10 million yearly payment due under its court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement, triggering enforcement of a debenture secured over assets of its key pharmaceutical subsidiary. The scheme, which had tied creditor recoveries to both fixed annual payments and a share of audited net profits through 2028, is now in default after the company failed to meet the 2025 payment deadline.

Following the default, the scheme administrator appointed Ernst & Young professionals as joint receivers and managers over the secured assets held by Toyot Pa Shun, the holding company for the group’s PRC pharmaceutical operations. The company is seeking legal advice and negotiating with the scheme administrator and receivers, warning that any disposal of these assets could lead to deconsolidation of its PRC subsidiaries and leave the group without sufficient operations, with trading in its shares remaining suspended.

More about Pa Shun International Holdings Limited

Pa Shun International Holdings Limited is an investment holding company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. Through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary Toyot Pa Shun Medicine Factory Company Limited, it controls two PRC units engaged in manufacturing and distributing pharmaceutical products, which currently contribute all of the group’s revenue.

data.title: Pharmaron Restructures H Share Awards Amid FX Policy Changes
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3759) ) has shared an update.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. has restructured its employee H share incentive arrangements to comply with changes in foreign exchange administrative policies affecting its share award schemes. The move reflects the company’s effort to maintain continuity of staff incentives while adapting to evolving regulatory requirements for Hong Kong-listed issuers.

The company’s management committee cancelled 5,396,470 outstanding H share awards previously granted to 546 employees under its First H Share Award and Trust Scheme. It then issued a replacement grant of the same number of H share awards to the same employees under the 2025 H Share Award and Trust Scheme on substantially identical terms, representing about 1.53% of issued H Shares and 0.29% of total shares.

The new grant, dated July 2, 2026, carries a nil grant price and was made when the H Shares closed at HK$18.85, preserving the economic value of the awards for participating employees. All replacement awards are conditional on the employees consenting to cancellation of the original grants, ensuring that the transition between schemes does not dilute their entitlements or disrupt the company’s incentive structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3759) stock is a Buy
with a HK$28.10 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H stock,
see the HK:3759 Stock Forecast page.

More about Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. is a China-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the pharmaceutical and life sciences sector. The company provides research, development, and related services, and uses H share award and trust schemes to incentivize its employees and align their interests with long-term corporate performance and market growth.

Its shares are traded as H Shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and the company manages multiple share award schemes approved by shareholders to support retention of key staff. These equity-based incentives form part of Pharmaron’s broader strategy to compete in global drug development and contract research markets.

Pharmaron’s capital structure includes both H Shares and domestic Shares, with H Share schemes representing a small portion of its total issued capital. The company’s governance framework empowers its board and management committees to implement and adjust share award plans in response to regulatory and market developments.

data.title: Shandong Boan Wins CDE Nod for Phase II Lung Cancer Trial of Novel Immunotherapy Combo
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Company., Limited. Class H ( (HK:6955) ) has provided an announcement.

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Co., Ltd. has secured approval from China’s Center for Drug Evaluation to commence a Phase II clinical trial of its anti-CD25 antibody BA1106 in combination with its PD-1 inhibitor BA1104 as a treatment for non-small cell lung cancer. The regimen targets driver gene-negative NSCLC patients in first- and second-line settings, a large segment of the lung cancer population that faces limited options once resistance to current immune checkpoint inhibitors develops.

BA1106 is the first investigational non-IL-2–blocking anti-CD25 antibody in China to enter solid tumor trials, designed to selectively deplete regulatory T cells while preserving IL-2 signaling and expanding effector T cells to boost anti-tumor immunity. Earlier Phase I data showed promising efficacy signals in several solid tumors and a favorable safety profile, and the new Phase II study will further assess efficacy and safety in immunotherapy-naïve and previously treated NSCLC patients while exploring broader use in other common solid tumors such as gastric cancer, potentially strengthening the company’s oncology portfolio and its role in next-generation immunotherapy.

More about Shandong Boan Biotechnology Company., Limited. Class H

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on innovative oncology therapies, including monoclonal antibodies and immuno-oncology drugs. The company targets major solid tumors such as non-small cell lung cancer and gastric cancer, aiming to address unmet clinical needs in patients who lack effective treatment options or have progressed on existing immunotherapies.

Its pipeline includes proprietary antibody candidates and biosimilar immune checkpoint inhibitors designed to enhance anti-tumor immune responses. By combining novel mechanisms like non-IL-2–blocking anti-CD25 antibodies with PD-1 inhibitors, Shandong Boan Biotechnology seeks to strengthen its position in next-generation cancer immunotherapy and compete in China’s rapidly evolving biopharma market.

Average Trading Volume: 6,146,470

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$2.91B

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. ( (HK:1518) ) just unveiled an announcement.

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. announced changes to its board, with non-executive director Mr. Wang Siye resigning effective June 30, 2026, citing work adjustments. The board noted that Mr. Wang has no disagreements with directors and expressed gratitude for his contributions, indicating a smooth and orderly transition in governance.

The company appointed Mr. Qiu Xiang as a new non-executive director effective July 2, 2026, bringing extensive healthcare investment experience and multiple directorships in medical and biotech firms. Mr. Qiu will serve an initial three-year term without remuneration, reinforcing the board’s focus on sector expertise and potentially strengthening New Century Healthcare’s strategic positioning and investor confidence.

The board stated that Mr. Qiu has no prior roles within the group, no relationships with existing management or major shareholders, and no shareholding in the company, underscoring his independence. This appointment aligns with listing rule requirements and highlights the firm’s attention to corporate governance, transparency, and oversight as it continues to operate in the competitive Chinese healthcare market.

More about New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd.

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. operates in the healthcare sector, providing medical services in China. The company is listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and is overseen by a board that includes executive and non-executive directors, reflecting its focus on governance and strategic oversight in a regulated market.

The group’s leadership structure incorporates non-executive directors from investment and healthcare-related backgrounds. This board composition underscores its emphasis on industry expertise and capital markets experience as it navigates the evolving Chinese healthcare landscape and regulatory environment.

data.title: LongBio Pharma Ends Global Offering Stabilization With Full Over-allotment Exercise
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An announcement from LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1779) ) is now available.

LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company incorporated in the PRC, has structured its capital raising through a Global Offering of H shares to investors outside the United States under Regulation S. The company’s listing involves both a Hong Kong Public Offering and an International Offering, reflecting its ambition to broaden its investor base and strengthen financial resources for its operations.

The company announced that the stabilization period for its Global Offering ended on July 2, 2026, the 30th day after the last date for lodging applications under the Hong Kong Public Offering. This marks the formal close of post-offering price support activities associated with its recent share sale.

During the stabilization period, Sinolink Securities (Hong Kong) Company Limited, acting as Stabilizing Manager, over-allocated 2,128,950 H shares in the International Offering, accounting for about 15% of the total offer shares initially available. This over-allocation mechanism is commonly used to support orderly trading and manage demand in the immediate aftermarket.

The Sole Sponsor-Overall Coordinator fully exercised the over-allotment option on June 30, 2026, in respect of the same 2,128,950 H shares at an offer price of HK$96.06 per share, excluding applicable fees and levies. This exercise allowed the company and its underwriters to facilitate delivery of H shares to placees who had agreed to delayed settlement under the Global Offering.

No purchases or sales of H shares were conducted in the open market by the Stabilizing Manager for the purpose of price stabilization during the stabilization period. The completion of stabilization without market trades suggests that aftermarket demand and trading in LongBio Pharma’s H shares may have been sufficiently robust to obviate the need for active price support.

The conclusion of the stabilization period and full exercise of the over-allotment option indicate that the Global Offering was fully subscribed and operationally successful. For stakeholders, including shareholders and prospective investors, the outcome provides clarity on the final size of the offering and underscores the company’s ability to execute a complex cross-border equity issuance within Hong Kong’s regulatory framework.

More about LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. Class H

LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China with limited liability and listed in Hong Kong as Class H shares under stock code 1779. The company operates in the pharmaceutical sector, focusing on the development and commercialization of biopharmaceutical products, and accesses international capital markets through global offerings of its H shares.

data.title: HKBN Revises China Mobile Linked Agreements and Caps After Year-End Change
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

HKBN ( (HK:1310) ) has shared an update.

HKBN has entered into 2026 supplemental agreements with CMHK and CMI to amend the scope of services under existing master agreements and renew their term, while revising annual transaction caps following a change in its financial year-end from 31 August to 31 December. These agreements cover a broad suite of services, including fixed-line, mobile backhaul, data centre and international telecommunications, to be provided between HKBN and the China Mobile Group.

Because CMHK and CMI are connected persons under Hong Kong Listing Rules, the amended arrangements and revised caps are treated as continuing connected transactions, triggering specific compliance requirements under Chapter 14A. Most services fall either into fully exempt or partially exempt categories, meaning they are subject to reporting, annual review and announcement obligations but do not require independent shareholder approval, and HKBN has appointed an independent financial adviser to confirm that multi-year terms exceeding three years are consistent with normal business practice.

More about HKBN

HKBN Ltd. is a Hong Kong-based telecommunications and technology services provider, offering fixed-line, mobile, data centre, international telecommunications and technology solutions. The company operates within the Hong Kong and Greater China connectivity markets, and is majority controlled by China Mobile through its subsidiary CMHK, which holds 74.84% of HKBN’s issued share capital.

HKBN’s services include fixed telecommunications network services, mobile backhaul, data centre hosting, international telecoms and consultancy-driven technology solutions for enterprise and carrier customers. Its close operational ties with the China Mobile Group position HKBN as a key partner in regional network infrastructure and cross-border connectivity services.
